The PTEN gene is located on the chromosome 10q11.2. About 95% of cases of Cowden syndrome are due to mutations in this gene. However, in some families, the syndrome is not due to a mutation in the PTEN gene but is instead due to errors in a different gene (called KLLN) that is very close to the PTEN gene. Other, rarer, genetic disorders that are similar to Cowden syndrome include tuberous sclerosis and Bannayan-Riley-Ruvalcaba syndrome.
